zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard le Boteler I於大約1222年出生在英國藍卡爾貝福 霍爾時，他的父親Alberic Pincerna le Boteler28歲，母親Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington23歲。他於大約1250年與Lady Alicia de Carleton在英國英格蘭藍卡爾特爾 勒 伍茲結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1281年在英國藍卡爾武特 洛可拉福享年60歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾聖 米可艾爾也斯 奧 維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard le Boteler I于大约1222年出生在英国藍卡爾貝福 霍爾时，他的父亲Alberic Pincerna le Boteler28岁，母亲Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington23岁。他于大约1250年与Lady Alicia de Carleton在英国英格兰藍卡爾特爾 勒 伍茲结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1281年在英国藍卡爾武特 洛可拉福享年60岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾聖 米可艾爾也斯 奧 維。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard le Boteler I was born about 1222, in Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England, his father, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, was 28 and his mother, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, was 23. He married Lady Alicia de Carleton about 1250, in Whittle le Woods, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1281, in Out Rawcliffe, Lancashire, England, at the age of 60, and was buried in St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard le Boteler I est né environ 1222, à Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lady Alicia de Carleton environ 1250, à Whittle le Woods, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1281, à Out Rawcliffe, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard le Boteler I um 1222 in Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alberic Pincerna le Boteler 28 und seine Mutter Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington 23. Er hat um 1250 in Whittle le Woods, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Lady Alicia de Carleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1281 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Out Rawcliffe, Lancashire, England und wurde in St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard le Boteler I è nato il circa 1222 nel Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Lady Alicia de Carleton circa 1250, nel Whittle le Woods, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1281, nel Out Rawcliffe, Lancashire, England, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard le Boteler Iが約1222年にイギリスランカシュアイアベウセイ ハルで生まれた時，父Alberic Pincerna le Botelerは28歳，母Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warringtonは23歳であった。約1250年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアウィットル ル ウッドスでLady Alicia de Carletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1281年にイギリスランカシュアイアオウト ラウクリフで60歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアスト ミチャエル'ス オン ウイアにて埋葬された。

ko.Whittle-Le-Woods, Lancashire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 휘틀 일 우드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard le Boteler I(이)가 대략 1222년 영국 랭카실르 뷰시 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alberic Pincerna le Boteler(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 휘틀 일 우드즈에서 Lady Alicia de Carleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1281년 영국 랭카실르 우트 롤클리프에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 스트 믹하얼'스  윈이르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard le Boteler I nasceu aproximadamente 1222, em Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Alicia de Carleton aproximadamente 1250, em Whittle le Woods, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1281, em Out Rawcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, Englan: ст мачаел'с он вир, ланкашир, Англия
ru.Whittle-Le-Woods, Lancashire, England: уиттл л вудс, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard le Boteler I родился около 1222 г. в местности бюси холл, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alberic Pincerna le Boteler было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Alicia de Carleton около 1250 г. в местности уиттл л вудс, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1281 г. в местности оут равклифф, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности ст мачаел'с он вир, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard le Boteler I nació aproximadamente 1222, en Bewsey Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Lady Alicia de Carleton aproximadamente 1250, en Whittle le Woods, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1281, en Out Rawcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en St Michael's on Wyre, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
